gloss_en: Are you well? How are you doing today?
notes: labās greeting, ki dāyr (harrell1962 §greetings; PADIC-MOROCCAN attests dāyr); initial cluster ʕl-

sentence: اَلْجَوّ زْوِين الْيُوم وَالشَّمْش طَالْعَة بِكْرِي.
raw: الجو زوين اليوم والشمش طالعة بكري.
ipa: alˈʒaww ˈzwiːn alˈjuːm waʃˈʃamʃ ˈtˤɑːlʕa ˈbəkriː
gloss_en: The weather is nice today and the sun rose early.
notes: zwīn 'nice' (harrell1962 s.v.); ج=/ʒ/ (heath2020); شمش Darija form with final š (harrell1962); bikri 'early'; short high /i/ reduces to schwa before a cluster — weak-position syncope (heath2020; watson2002)

sentence: مَا بْغِيتْش قَهْوَة، بْغِيت أَتَاي بِالنَّعْنَاع.
raw: ما بغيتش قهوة، بغيت أتاي بالنعناع.
ipa: ˈmaː ˈbɣiːtʃ ˈqahwa ˈbɣiːt ʔaˈtaːj binnaʕˈnaːʕ
gloss_en: I don't want coffee, I want tea with mint.
notes: circumfix negation ma-...-š (harrell1962 §negation); bɣīt 'I want'; atāy 'tea' (Darija); ق=/q/ urban (noamane2020)

sentence: شْرِيت تْلَاتَة كِيلُو مَاطِيشَة مِن السُّوق بِعَشْرَة دْرَاهِم.
raw: شريت تلاتة كيلو ماطيشة من السوق بعشرة دراهم.
ipa: ˈʃriːt ˈtlaːta ˈkiːluː mɑːˈtˤiːʃa ˈmin asˈsuːq biˈʕaʃra ˈdraːhim
gloss_en: I bought three kilos of tomatoes from the market for ten dirhams.
notes: maṭīša 'tomato' (Darija lexeme, harrell1962); ث→/t/ tlāta (heath2020: stop reflexes); initial clusters šr-/tl-/dr-

sentence: هَادْ الرَّاجِل خْدَّام بْزَّاف وْمَا كَيْگْلَسْش فِي الظُّهْر.
raw: هاد الراجل خدام بزاف وما كيگلسش في الظهر.
ipa: ˈhaːd arˈraːʒil ˈxddaːm ˈbzzaːf ˈwmaː kajɡˈlasʃ ˈfiː dˤˈdˤuhr
gloss_en: This man works a lot and doesn't sit down at noon.
notes: bzzāf 'a lot' (harrell1962); گ=/ɡ/ in kayglas (Darija g, heath2020); durative ka-/kay- (ennaji2004); ma-...-š

gloss_en: Go to the market and buy the bread, please.
notes: Darija imperatives sir/shri (harrell1962); heavy onset clusters from vowel syncope; ʕafak politeness tag

sentence: الْيُوم الْجَوّ بَارْد وْمَا كَايْنْش الشّْتَا.
raw: اليوم الجو بارد وما كاينش الشتا.
ipa: alˈjuːm alˈʒaww ˈbaːrd ˈwmaː ˈkaːjnʃ ˈaʃʃtaː
gloss_en: Today the weather is cold and there is no rain.
notes: bard 'cold', existential negation ma kayn-š (harrell1962 §negation); š-šta 'the rain' sun-assimilated (shadda on sīn); w-ma proclitic before consonant

gloss_en: How much is this bread? Two dirhams.
notes: bšḥal 'how much', juj 'two' (Darija numeral, harrell1962); ق=/q/ urban reflex (noamane2020); initial clusters bʒ-/dr-; short high /i/ reduces to schwa before a cluster — weak-position syncope (heath2020; watson2002)

gloss_en: I have an appointment with the doctor at three.
notes: ʕndi 'I have', mʕa 'with', ṭbib 'doctor'; f-t-tlata 'at three' with ث→t stop reflex (heath2020); ط emphatic

gloss_en: My brother lives in Rabat with the family.
notes: xuya 'my brother', durative prefix ka-/kay- on the imperfect (ennaji2004); ʕayla 'family'; emphatic ṛ in Rbaṭ raises ā

sentence: دُوز مِنْ هْنَا وْدُور عَ الْيْمِين.
raw: دوز من هنا ودور ع اليمين.
ipa: ˈduːz ˈmən ˈhnaː ˈwduːr ˈʕa ljˈmiːn
gloss_en: Pass by here and turn to the right.
notes: Darija imperatives duz 'pass', dur 'turn' (harrell1962); hna 'here' (Darija, not MSA hunā); ʕa + article proclitic; short high /i/ reduces to schwa before a cluster — weak-position syncope (heath2020; watson2002)

gloss_en: The food was very tasty yesterday.
notes: makla 'food', bnin/bnina 'tasty', bzzaf 'a lot', l-bariḥ 'yesterday' (harrell1962); geminate zz

gloss_en: Where did they go? They didn't come today.
notes: fin 'where', mšaw 'they went', circumfix negation ma-…-š (harrell1962); ج=/ʒ/ fricative (heath2020)

gloss_en: How many children do you have?
notes: šḥal mn 'how many' governs a singular count noun (harrell1962); wld 'child/son'; pharyngeal ḥ; short high /i/ reduces to schwa before a cluster — weak-position syncope (heath2020; watson2002)

sentence: إِلَا جِيتِي غَدَّا، نْمْشِيو مْعَ بْعْضِيَّتْنَا.
raw: إلا جيتي غدا، نمشيو مع بعضيتنا.
ipa: ˈʔilaː ˈʒiːtiː ˈɣaddaː ˈnmʃiːuː ˈmʕa bʕdˤijˈjatnaː
gloss_en: If you come tomorrow, we will go together.
notes: ila 'if' conditional, jiti 'you came', ghadda 'tomorrow'; n-…-u 1pl imperfect nmšiw (ennaji2004); bʕḍiyyatna 'together'; geminate jj

sentence: الْبَارِح مْشِيت لْلْمْدِينَة وْشْرِيت جَلَّابَة جْدِيدَة.
raw: البارح مشيت للمدينة وشريت جلابة جديدة.
ipa: alˈbaːriħ ˈmʃiːt ˈllmdiːna ˈwʃriːt ʒalˈlaːba ˈʒdiːda
gloss_en: Yesterday I went to the old city and bought a new jellaba.
notes: mšit 1sg past, jellaba 'robe', jdida 'new'; heavy onset clusters mš-/šr-/jd- from short-vowel syncope (heath2020); geminate ll

gloss_en: I am going to travel to France next summer.
notes: ghadi future particle + n- imperfect (ennaji2004); ṣ-ṣif 'the summer' sun-assimilated; l-jay 'coming/next'; short high /i/ reduces to schwa before a cluster — weak-position syncope (heath2020; watson2002)

gloss_en: Call me on the phone when you arrive.
notes: ʕayyaṭ 'call' imperative, mnin 'when', tuṣal 'you arrive' (harrell1962); geminate jj/ll; ط emphatic

sentence: السّْلَامْ عْلِيكُم، أَشْ خْبَارَك؟ الْحَمْدُ لِلّاه كُلْشِي مْزْيَان.
raw: السلام عليكم، أش خبارك؟ الحمد للاه كلشي مزيان.
ipa: assˈlaːm ˈʕliːkum ˈʔaʃ ˈxbaːrak alˈħamdu lilˈlaːh ˈkulʃiː ˈmzjaːn
gloss_en: Peace be upon you, how are you? Praise God, everything is fine.
notes: s-slam ʕlikum + response pair; ash xbarak 'how are you', kulshi mzyan 'everything fine' (harrell1962); frozen ḥamdu-lillah formula

gloss_en: What has passed is dead.
notes: Darija proverb lli fat mat; relative particle əlli (harrell1962); ث→t / stop reflexes throughout
